description: End-to-end gate for the open-vocab rebuild (WP-I). 'expected' is the plan's acceptance target; 'recorded' is the real verification result on prod; 'gaps' documents the honest shortfalls against the target.
address_note: Proxy for '3 BLACK SWAN RISE BEELIAR' which is missing from G-NAF.

recorded.method: synth_property_facts against the real Beeliar parcel (cadastre 1585770); live auto-resolution is licence-gated so the parcel was bound directly to isolate the spatial+engine path.

gaps: pass_or_fail (2) < target 10: synth provides LOT MEASUREMENTS (area/width/depth); setbacks, heights, garage, etc. require PROPOSED design values from a drawing upload and correctly return needs_more_info. The >=10 target is unreachable from spatial synth alone — it needs a proposal/drawing.

Auto-derived check PRECISION is limited: the clustering used the deterministic token-normalisation fallback (sentence-transformers/hdbscan are not installed), so some canonical clusters are heterogeneous (e.g. 'site_area' aggregates min-lot-size, max-area and exception rules), which can pick a wrong threshold (the site_area likely_fail above is such a case). Fix: install the ML clustering deps and run WP-D cluster review + WP-E adversarial review.

LIVE auto-resolution for Beeliar is blocked by dataset governance: Landgate cadastre (lgate-001) and DPLH planning (dplh-*) are approval_status=pending_review with restricted licence ('commercial use unconfirmed'). The resolver correctly refuses non-approved restricted data. Approving them is an operator legal decision; once approved, resolution runs the same synth proven here.
